column1: # mapping_set_title: Open Policy Agent (OPA) to NIST OSCAL Mappings
# mapping_set_description: Schema-level mappings between Open Policy Agent (OPA)
#   LinkML classes and NIST OSCAL (Open Security Controls Assessment Language)
#   document model classes (catalog, profile, component, system-security-plan).
#   OSCAL is the canonical machine-readable language for control catalogs,
#   profiles, and component-level control implementations; OPA bundles are a
#   natural fit for OSCAL component-definition and implemented-requirement
#   semantics because they package executable policies that implement
#   controls. Predicates - skos:closeMatch where the two classes describe the
#   same governance artefact (e.g. bundle Manifest as an OSCAL DefinedComponent,
#   engine Capabilities as an OSCAL Capability); skos:relatedMatch where the
#   OPA class realises only one part of an OSCAL concept (e.g. WasmResolver as
#   one possible implementation path for an OSCAL ImplementedRequirement);
#   skos:broadMatch where the OPA class is a container that wraps multiple
#   OSCAL artefacts (e.g. Manifest carrying many component definitions, or
#   BuiltinMetadataCatalog grouping multiple built-in function descriptions).

column10: A signed OPA bundle Manifest packages a discrete, deployable policy capability - semantically the same governance artefact as an OSCAL DefinedComponent
A Manifest may bundle multiple roots and wasm resolvers, each representing a defined component; the manifest container broadly subsumes a ComponentDefinition document
A WasmResolver binds a policy path to a wasm-compiled entrypoint - one realisation path for an OSCAL implemented-requirement, but only one of many delivery vehicles
A resolver entrypoint implements an executable statement of a control's enforcement logic, parallel to an OSCAL implemented-control-statement
An IR Policy is a curated selection of evaluation rules akin to an OSCAL Profile selecting and tailoring controls from a catalog
Each AuthorizationInput defines the subject/action/resource frame that the engine evaluates - the runtime analogue of an OSCAL system authorization boundary
OPA Capabilities describe the engine's supported features and built-ins - directly comparable to an OSCAL Capability describing a component's offered capability surface
A built-in metadata catalog is a structured, versioned catalog of capability descriptions; broadly aligned with the OSCAL Catalog document type
A BuiltinCategoryGroup organises built-ins under a thematic heading (crypto, http, regex) - parallel to an OSCAL Group organising controls under a family heading
A Rego built-in is an atomic, named primitive available to policies; partially analogous to an OSCAL Control as a named, addressable governance unit
